Insidious: The Red Door (2023)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $82,156,962Details
International Box Office $104,101,966
Worldwide Box Office $186,258,928
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,366,565
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,390,356
Total $2,756,921
Further financial details...

Synopsis

To put their demons to rest once and for all, Josh and a college-aged Dalton must go deeper into The Further than ever before, facing their family’s dark past and a host of new and more horrifying terrors that lurk behind the red door.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$33,013,036 (40.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.49 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:44.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$16,000,000 (worldwide box office is 11.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,188 opening theaters/3,188 max. theaters, 3.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $89,816,933

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 7th, 2023 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: July 5th, 2023 (Wide) (France)
July 6th, 2023 (Wide) (Australia)
July 6th, 2023 (Wide), released as Insidious 5 (Germany)
July 6th, 2023 (Wide), released as Insidious 5 (Mexico)
July 6th, 2023 (Wide), released as Insidious La Porta Rossa (Italy)
Video Release: August 1st, 2023 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for violence, terror, frightening images, strong language and suggestive references.
(Rating bulletin 2788 (cert #54484), 6/21/2023)
Running Time: 107 minutes
Franchise: Insidious
Cast, crew, or production detail: Legacy Sequel
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (state or county): New Jersey, United States
Plot point: Haunting
Social setting: Supernatural
Subgenre: Supernatural Horror
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Horror
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Blumhouse, Screen Gems, Stage 6 Films
Production Countries: Canada, United States
Languages: English
